findings.evidence: Mathlib has `Quotient.lift_surjective` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Quot.lean:312`; the new map is definitionally `Quotient.lift p (orbitRel_le_ker_proj (p := p))` at `Deck.lean:131-132`.
findings.fix: Remove this lemma or make downstream uses call `Quotient.lift_surjective p (orbitRel_le_ker_proj (p := p)) hp` directly.
findings.issue: `orbitRelQuotientProj_surjective` is just Mathlib's generic surjectivity theorem for `Quotient.lift` specialized to `orbitRelQuotientProj`.

findings.evidence: Mathlib has `Setoid.lift_injective_iff_ker_eq_of_le` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Setoid/Basic.lean:358`; the new proof at `Deck.lean:150-154` applies exactly that theorem to `r = MulAction.orbitRel (Deck p) E`.
findings.fix: Expose the fibre-orbit hypothesis as `Setoid.ker p = MulAction.orbitRel (Deck p) E` and use `Setoid.lift_injective_iff_ker_eq_of_le` at call sites instead of adding a specialized injectivity lemma.
findings.issue: `orbitRelQuotientProj_injective_of_fiber_subset_orbit` creates a deck-specific parallel form of the generic kernel criterion for quotient lifts.

summary: The deck-specific quotient projection is reasonable, but the PR adds parallel special-case API for generic quotient-lift facts that Mathlib already provides.
